que: What is a fragment in Android?
options: A reusable portion of app UI

que: Can a fragment exist completely on its own without a host?
options: No, it must be hosted by an activity or another fragment

que: Why are fragments useful according to Android docs?
options: They add modularity and reusability to activity UI
They replace activities in the manifest entirely
They are only for Wear OS

que: Which Jetpack library commonly works with fragments?
What is a common fragment mistake?
options: Using fragments as modular UI units
Keeping fragments focused on UI concerns
Putting too much business logic in fragment classes

que: What attaches to the host's view hierarchy?
Which statement about fragment lifecycle is true?
options: It is distinct from the host activity lifecycle
It is identical to the activity lifecycle in every detail
Fragments do not have lifecycle concerns
Fragments only exist in Compose apps

que: What is a simple valid fragment declaration?
Which official docs best support this topic?
options: Android fragments docs and navigation testing docs
